rulings.Case Title: ADAM SMITH ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING REGARDING PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 16, 2023 line 1. HEARING REGARDING PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is DENIED. The Board's findings are supported by the record: that (1) conversion is accompanied by a 10-20% rent increase which provides somewhat increased housing accessibility to middle income residents. (2) removal of these TIC units reduces the diversity of unit types and is inconsistent with Policy 3.3; (3) removal of these TIC units reduces the diversity of unit types, and is inconsistent with Policy 5.4; (4) conversion exacerbates the inaccessibility of homeownership in the North Beach neighborhood; (5) the Tentative Map is not consistent with the General Plan; (6) conversion does not promote the health, safety, and welfare of the City; and (7) the Tentative Map application contained factual discrepancies, inconsistencies, and/or incorrect information regarding the building's short-term rental and eviction history. Petitioners' equal protection argument fails. Petitioners did not establish that the existence of similarly situated persons, that the difference in treatment was intentional, or that there was no rational basis for the difference in treatment. Respondent's request for judicial notice of Supervisors Ordinance No. 117-13, the Condominium Conversion Fee: Economic Impact Report, and San Francisco's Condominium Conversion Nexus Analysis is granted pursuant to Evidence Code sections 452(b), (h). Petitioners' request for judicial notice of the General Plan, Housing Element, A copy of Owens v. City and County of San Francisco, Minutes of Planning Commission Hearing of September 24, 2020 and January 23, 2020, and the Planning Commissions Motions Nos. 20785 and 20634 are granted pursuant to Evidence Code sections 452(d), (h). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ZHIJUN WU VS. JIANBO ZHONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Tax Or Strike Cost And Notice Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 16, 2023 line 4. CROSS-DEFENDANT ZHIJUN WU'S MOTION TO TAX OR STRIKE COSTS is OFF CALENDAR. Motion fails to comply with CRC 3.1110. The body of the Notice of Motion appears to be for a "Special Motion to Strike," that is not the motion before the Court per the Motion and the Points and Authorities. The motion is additionally moot. The untimely Memorandum of Costs filed before the remittitur is stricken on the Court's own motion. The remittitur issued on May 30, 2023 was filed on June 6, 2023. CRC 8.278, Furthermore, it has come to the Court's attention that the Court has previously heard and ruled on a Judgment on the Pleadings. The Court's order associated with the hearing is hereby vacated as null and void. No remittitur on file at the time of the hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: 672 CASTRO STREET LLC ET AL VS. JEANINE REISBI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 16, 2023 line 7. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / ADJUDICATION Hearing Required in part and denied in part. Hearing Requires as to the Motion for Summary Judgment to address the following: (1) The date(s) of the following event "672 Castro Street LLC representatives told us we could not go into the basement or put anything there" referenced in Defendants' declarations in paragraph 12; (2) for Defendants to identify evidence submitted in opposition to the motion supporting the following contention "as part of the their lease agreement established with the Plaintiff's, Defendants had at the Premises Wi-Fi services." Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied without prejudice for Plaintiff to file an new motion briefing each defense separately and for defendant to address each defense separately. Briefs submitted by both sides group the defenses for discussion (notably sides do not include the same defenses in the same group, e.g. defense #24 is grouped with 1 and 25 by the Plaintiff and Defendants group it with 1-9 and 34 for discussion); do not quote the defense discussed sometimes referencing the defense by number and sometimes by title; and it also appears that some defenses while listed in the subheading are not mentioned, discussed or analyzed in any way in the body of the argument. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
